markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Chain](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=1)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 81

**Favored Weapon**
[Flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=19)

</column>

Members of the Order of the Chain are skilled at hunting down and capturing criminals, especially those who dare flee from justice.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Shackles of Law
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 81

</column>

You attempt to subdue a target with an attack and a follow-up grab. Make a melee Strike. If it hits and deals damage, you can attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to Grapple the creature you hit. If you’re wielding a flail, you can ignore Grapple’s requirement that you have a hand free. Both attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn’t increase until after you’ve made both of them.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Sturdy Bindings
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 81

</column>

Your grapples are incredibly reliable. When you roll a critical failure on a check to Grapple a target, you get a failure instead. In addition, when a creature you have grabbed rolls a failure on its check to Escape, it gets a critical failure instead, and if it rolls a critical success, it gets a success instead.
name: Order of the Chain

---

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Godclaw](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=10)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

**Favored Weapon**
[Morningstar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=363)

</column>

The creed of the Order of the Godclaw is “Righteousness by obedience.” These Hellknights extol the virtues of a pantheon of imposing deities.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Dedication to the Five
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

</column>

Your dedication to the Godclaw borders on a cleric's. You gain the cleric's [Domain Initiate](/Feats.aspx?ID=4644) feat but must select [duty](/Domains.aspx?ID=108), [might](/Domains.aspx?ID=83), [perfection](/Domains.aspx?ID=89), or [protection](/Domains.aspx?ID=90) as your domain. Whenever you cast the focus spell gained from this feat, you gain temporary Hit Points equal to twice the spell's rank that last for 1 round. In addition, you can use any religious symbol of [Abadar](/Deities.aspx?ID=277), [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278), [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=285), [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=286), or [Uirch](/Deities.aspx?ID=560) as your divine focus.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Blessing of the Five
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

**Frequency**
once per day

</column>

You call out to the Godclaw to heal your allies. You cast the 3-action version of [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), heightened to a rank one lower than half your level rounded up. You can select up to five creatures in the area to remain unaffected by the spell. You can instead use this ability to bring a creature within 30 feet that has died within the last round back from the dead. If you do, the creature is restored to 1 Hit Point and is [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1.
name: Order of the Godclaw

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Nail](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=11)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

**Favored Weapons**
[Halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=381), [Lance](/Weapons.aspx?ID=383)

</column>

Order of the Nail The creed of the Order of the Nail is “Savagery must bequelled, in the land, home, and mind.” These Hellknights work to tame wild frontier regions.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Trailblazing Stride
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

</column>

While moving on land, you ignore any [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) caused by plants, fungi, or uneven ground. When you succeed at a skill check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about a creature with the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531), [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=547), [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614), or [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) trait, you gain a +10-foot circumstance bonus to your Speeds for the rest of your turn.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Reveal Beasts
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

</column>

You're adept at revealing—or creating—fear among those who threaten civilization. If you succeed at a check to Recall Knowledge to identify an animal, beast, fungus, or plant, you get a critical success instead. Additionally, whenever you critically succeed at a check to Recall Knowledge against an animal, beast, fungus, or plant, that creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
name: Order of the Nail

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Pyre](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=12)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

**Favored Weapon**
[Glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=375)

</column>

The creed of the Order of the Pyre is “Reason’s flame consumes the shadow of corruption.” These Hellknights seek to purge what they call blasphemous ideologies from the world.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Righteous Resistance
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 29

</column>

You can cast [_resist energy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651) as a divine innate spell twice per day, except you can target only yourself. If you grant yourself resistance to fire damage, the resistance is equal to either the spell's normal value or to your level, whichever is higher.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Spiritual Disruption
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 29

</column>

You channel disruptive power that severs a foe's spiritual connection to their magic. Make a melee Strike. This counts as two attacks when calculating your multiple attack penalty. If the attack hits, the target takes 1d6 [persistent spirit damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The target is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 until it stops taking the persistent spirit damage.
name: Order of the Pyre

<additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Rack](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=13)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

**Favored Weapons**
[Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386), [Whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=404)

</column>

The creed of the Order of the Rack is “The venoms of the mind poison the body.” These Hellknights suppress rebellions, usually within Cheliax.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Disillusionment
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 29

</column>

Dreams are pointless illusions you seek to quash. If you roll a success on a saving throw against an [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) or [dream](/Traits.aspx?ID=582) effect or a Perception check to disbelieve an illusion, you get a critical success instead. If you roll a critical failure on any of those checks, you get a failure instead.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Silence Heresy
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 29

</column>

You channel a power to stifle dangerous words. Make a melee Strike. If the attack hits, the target is unable to speak in a voice louder than a whisper for 1 round. This prevents the target from using most [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) effects involving its voice, and it must succeed at a DC 5 flat check to Cast a Spell or the spell is disrupted. If your Strike is a critical hit, the target is entirely unable to speak for 1 round and is limited to a whisper for 1 minute thereafter. Once a creature has been affected by this ability, it's temporarily immune for 1 minute.
name: Order of the Rack
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28
</additional-info>

---

<summary>
The creed of the Order of the Rack is “The venoms of the mind poison the body.” These Hellknights suppress rebellions, usually within Cheliax.
</summary>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Scourge](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=14)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

**Favored Weapons**
[Mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=362), [Whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=404), [Scourge](/Weapons.aspx?ID=531)

</column>

The creed of the Order of the Scourge is “Without culpability, chaos reigns.” These Hellknights root out corruption in all its forms.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Fear No Law, Fear No One
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 29

</column>

When your allies see you stand up to fear, they become similarly emboldened. Make a Strike. If you hit and deal damage, each ally within a 20-foot emanation around you who can see you reduces the value of their [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition by 1. If the target of your Strike was the source of their frightened condition, they reduce the value by 2 instead.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Limited"
>
Seek Injustice
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 29

**Frequency**
once per day

</column>

With a moment spent focusing, you can locate the telltale signs of corruption no matter what means it might use to hide from your sight. For 1 minute, you can see through up to 5 feet of stone, wood, or similar barriers as if they didn't exist (though any amount of metal or denser barriers blocks this effect).
name: Order of the Scourge
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28
</additional-info>

---

<summary>
The creed of the Order of the Scourge is “Without culpability, chaos reigns.” These Hellknights root out corruption in all its forms.
</summary>
summary: The creed of the Order of the Scourge is “Without culpability, chaos reigns.” These Hellknights root out corruption in all its forms.

Make a Strike. If you hit and deal damage, each ally within a 20-foot emanation around you who can see you reduces the value of their frightened condition by 1. If the target of your Strike was the source of their frightened condition, they reduce the value by 2 instead. <title level="2" right="Greater Order Benefit" pfs="Limited" > Seek Injustice Three Actions Source Hellfire Dispatches pg. 29 Frequency once per day With a moment spent focusing, you can locate the telltale signs of corruption no matter what means it might use to hide from your sight. For 1 minute, you can see through up to 5 feet of stone, wood, or similar barriers as if they didn't exist (though any amount of metal or denser barriers blocks this effect).

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Gate](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=2)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 81

**Favored Weapon**
[Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

The Hellknight signifers of the Order of the Gate seek to prevent lawlessness of all types through the use of their considerable spellcasting prowess and command of fiendish outsiders.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Locate Lawbreakers
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 81

</column>

Your pursuit is relentless, and you are able to magically locate stolen objects and outlaws. You gain [_locate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=173) as an innate spell of a tradition of your choice, which you can cast once per day. If you are a member of the Order of the Gate, when you reach 14th level, the spell is heightened to 5th level.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Devil Allies
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

</column>

You can call in devils to assist your efforts. You gain [_summon fiend_](/Spells.aspx?ID=323) as an innate spell of a tradition of your choice, which you can cast once per day. When you cast this spell, you can summon only creatures that have the devil trait. At 16th level and every 2 levels thereafter, the spell is heightened by 1 level (to a maximum of 8th level when you reach 20th level).
name: Order of the Gate
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 81
</additional-info>

---

<summary>
The Hellknight signifers of the Order of the Gate seek to prevent lawlessness of all types through the use of their considerable spellcasting prowess …
</summary>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Godclaw](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=3)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

**Favored Weapon**
[Morningstar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

The Order of the Godclaw’s Hellknights see their cause as a holy mandate from the Godclaw. They seek to punish criminals with a zeal rarely matched by others who enforce the law.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Dedication to the Five
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

</column>

Your dedication to the Godclaw borders on a cleric’s. You gain the cleric’s [Domain Initiate](/Feats.aspx?ID=265) feat but must select [perfection](/Domains.aspx?ID=26), [protection](/Domains.aspx?ID=27), [tyranny](/Domains.aspx?ID=33), or [zeal](/Domains.aspx?ID=37) as your domain. In addition, you can use any religious symbol of [Abadar](/Deities.aspx?ID=1), [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=2), [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=9), [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=10), or [Torag](/Deities.aspx?ID=18) as your divine focus.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Blessing of the Five
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

**Frequency**
once per day

</column>

You call out to the Godclaw to heal your allies. You cast the 3-action version of [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), heightened to a level 1 lower than half your level rounded up. You can select up to four creatures in the area to remain unaffected by the spell. You can instead use this ability to bring a creature within 30 feet that has died within the last round back from the dead. If you do, the creature is restored to 1 Hit Point and is doomed 1 for 24 hours.
name: Order of the Godclaw
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82
</additional-info>

---

<summary>
The Order of the Godclaw’s Hellknights see their cause as a holy mandate from the Godclaw. They seek to punish criminals with a zeal rarely matched …
</summary>
summary: The Order of the Godclaw’s Hellknights see their cause as a holy mandate from the Godclaw. They seek to punish criminals with a zeal rarely matched …
summary_markdown: The Order of the Godclaw’s Hellknights see their cause as a holy mandate from the Godclaw. They seek to punish criminals with a zeal rarely matched …
text: Order of the Godclaw Source Character Guide pg. 82 Favored Weapon Morningstar The Order of the Godclaw’s Hellknights see their cause as a holy mandate from the Godclaw. They seek to punish criminals with a zeal rarely matched by others who enforce the law. <title level="2" right="Lesser Order Benefit" pfs="Standard" > Dedication to the Five Source Character Guide pg. 82 Your dedication to the Godclaw borders on a cleric’s. You gain the cleric’s Domain Initiate feat but must select perfection, protection, tyranny, or zeal as your domain. In addition, you can use any religious symbol of Abadar, Asmodeus, Iomedae, Irori, or Torag as your divine focus. <title level="2" right="Greater Order Benefit" pfs="Standard" > Blessing of the Five Three Actions Source Character Guide pg. 82 Frequency once per day You call out to the Godclaw to heal your allies. You cast the 3-action version of heal , heightened to a level 1 lower than half your level rounded up. You can select up to four creatures in the area to remain unaffected by the spell. You can instead use this ability to bring a creature within 30 feet that has died within the last round back from the dead. If you do, the creature is restored to 1 Hit Point and is doomed 1 for 24 hours.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Nail](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=4)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

**Favored Weapons**
[Halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=26), [Lance](/Weapons.aspx?ID=28)

</column>

Members of the Order of the Nail see civilized society as an ideal and seek to unite all people within their cultural baseline. They also seek to destroy anyone who does not accept Chelaxian culture along with the wild beasts that threaten the safety of civilization.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Trailblazing Stride
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

</column>

You are used to moving through the wilderness. While moving on land, you ignore the effects of non-magical difficult terrain.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Reveal Beasts
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=606" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 82

</column>

You are adept at revealing—or creating—weaknesses among those who are a threat to civilization. If you roll a success on a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) to identify an animal or a beast, you get a critical success instead. The next time you damage that creature, it gains weakness 5 during that attack to one damage type of your choice that you dealt it.
name: Order of the Nail

<additional-info>

</additional-info>

While moving on land, you ignore the effects of non-magical difficult terrain. <title level="2" right="Greater Order Benefit" pfs="Standard" > Reveal Beasts Source Character Guide pg. 82 You are adept at revealing—or creating—weaknesses among those who are a threat to civilization. If you roll a success on a check to Recall Knowledge to identify an animal or a beast, you get a critical success instead. The next time you damage that creature, it gains weakness 5 during that attack to one damage type of your choice that you dealt it.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Pyre](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=5)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

**Favored Weapon**
[Glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=20)

</column>

The Order of the Pyre is dedicated to eliminating those faiths and beliefs they consider to be a threat to the Inner Sea region. They are particularly keen on stamping out dangerous cults.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Righteous Resistance
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

</column>

You can cast [_resist energy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=256) twice per day as a divine innate spell, except you can target only yourself. If you grant yourself resistance to fire damage, the resistance is equal to either the spell’s normal value or to your level, whichever is higher.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Spiritual Disruption
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=623" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=645" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

</column>

You channel disruptive power that severs a foe’s spiritual connection to their heretical magic. Make a melee Strike. If the attack hits, any time the target attempts to cast a divine or occult spell, the spell is [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=394) unless they succeed at a DC 5 flat check (DC 7 on a critical hit); if the target is also [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37), it must attempt the harder of the two flat checks (but not both). This effect lasts for 1 minute.
name: Order of the Pyre
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83
</additional-info>

---

<summary>
The Order of the Pyre is dedicated to eliminating those faiths and beliefs they consider to be a threat to the Inner Sea region. They are …
</summary>

If you grant yourself resistance to fire damage, the resistance is equal to either the spell’s normal value or to your level, whichever is higher. <title level="2" right="Greater Order Benefit" pfs="Standard" > Spiritual Disruption Two Actions Source Character Guide pg. 83 You channel disruptive power that severs a foe’s spiritual connection to their heretical magic. Make a melee Strike. If the attack hits, any time the target attempts to cast a divine or occult spell, the spell is disrupted unless they succeed at a DC 5 flat check (DC 7 on a critical hit); if the target is also stupefied, it must attempt the harder of the two flat checks (but not both). This effect lasts for 1 minute.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Rack](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=6)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

**Favored Weapons**
[Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31), [Whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48)

</column>

Members of the Order of the Rack seek to eliminate wastefulness throughout the Inner Sea region. They seek to quash pointless dreams, quell rebellions, and destroy dangerous inventions.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Disillusionment
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

</column>

Dreams are pointless illusions you seek to quash. If you roll a success on a saving throw against an illusion or dream effect or a Perception check to disbelieve an illusion, you get a critical success instead. If you roll a critical failure on any of those checks, you get a failure instead.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Silence Heresy
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

</column>

You channel a power that stifles dangerous words. Make a melee Strike. If the attack hits, the target is unable to speak in a voice louder than a whisper for 1 round. This prevents the target from using most auditory effects involving its voice, and it must succeed at a DC 5 flat check to Cast a Spell with a verbal component or the spell is disrupted. If your Strike is a critical hit, the target is entirely unable to speak for 1 round and is limited to a whisper for 1 minute thereafter. Once a creature has been affected by this ability, it is temporarily immune for 1 minute.
name: Order of the Rack
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83
</additional-info>

---

<summary>
Members of the Order of the Rack seek to eliminate wastefulness throughout the Inner Sea region. They seek to quash pointless dreams, quell …
</summary>

This prevents the target from using most auditory effects involving its voice, and it must succeed at a DC 5 flat check to Cast a Spell with a verbal component or the spell is disrupted. If your Strike is a critical hit, the target is entirely unable to speak for 1 round and is limited to a whisper for 1 minute thereafter. Once a creature has been affected by this ability, it is temporarily immune for 1 minute.

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Scourge](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=7)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

**Favored Weapons**
[Mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=7), [Scourge](/Weapons.aspx?ID=89), [Whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48)

</column>

Hellknights of the Order of the Scourge hunt those who pervert the law for their own selfish ends. They seek to destroy corruption and those who hide behind unjust laws and bureaucracy.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Fear No Law, Fear No One
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

</column>

When you roll a success on a save against a fear effect, you get a critical success instead. In addition, any time you gain the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition, reduce the condition’s value by 1. As usual, this ability reduces the initial [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition by 1, so if you have multiple similar abilities, they don’t both take effect.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Seek Injustice
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=590" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=606" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
<trait label="Revelation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=138" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=727" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83

**Frequency**
once per day

</column>

With a moment spent focusing, you can locate the telltale signs of corruption no matter what means it may use to hide from your sight. For 1 minute, you can see through up to 5 feet of stone, wood, or similar barriers as if they didn’t exist (though any amount of metal or denser barriers block this effect).
name: Order of the Scourge
search_markdown:
<additional-info>
**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 83
</additional-info>

---

<summary>
Hellknights of the Order of the Scourge hunt those who pervert the law for their own selfish ends. They seek to destroy corruption and those who hide …
</summary>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Chain](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=8)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

**Favored Weapons**
[Flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=374), [Spiked Chain](/Weapons.aspx?ID=417)

</column>

The creed of the Order of the Chain is “All men lift themselves up upon the backs of others.” These Hellknights seek to punish those who actively impede what they believe to be civilization’s progress.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Shackles of Law
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

</column>

([flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=606)) You attempt to subdue a target with an attack and a follow-up grab. Make a melee Strike. If it hits and deals damage, you can attempt an Athletics check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) the creature you hit. If you're wielding a flail or spiked chain, you can ignore Grapple's requirement that you have a hand free, and if you succeed at the Athletics check, you get a critical success instead. Both attacks count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after you've made both of them.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Sturdy Bindings
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

</column>

Your grapples are incredibly reliable. When you roll a critical failure on a check to Grapple a target, you get a failure instead. In addition, when a creature you have [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) rolls a failure on its check to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296), it gets a critical failure instead, and if it rolls a critical success, it gets a success instead.
name: Order of the Chain

</additional-info>

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Hellknight Order " pfs="Standard">[Order of the Gate](/HellknightOrders.aspx?ID=9)</title>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

**Favored Weapon**
[Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)

</column>

The creed of the Order of the Gate is “Judgment in the face of depravity.” These Hellknights seek to prevent crime through magic and extraplanar knowledge.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Lesser Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Locate Lawbreakers
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

</column>

Your pursuit is relentless, and you can magically locate stolen objects and outlaws. You gain [_locate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1588) as an innate spell of a tradition of your choice, which you can cast once per day. If you're a member of the Order of the Gate, when you reach 14th level, the spell is heightened to 5th rank.

<title
    level="2"
    right="Greater Order Benefit"
    pfs="Standard"
>
Devil Allies
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Hellfire Dispatches](/Sources.aspx?ID=319) pg. 28

</column>

You can call forth devils to assist your efforts in preventing criminal acts. You gain [_summon fiend_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1701) as a 6th-rank innate spell of a tradition of your choice, which you can cast once per day. When you cast this spell, you can summon only creatures that have the [devil](/Traits.aspx?ID=575) trait. At 14th level and every 2 levels thereafter, the spell is heightened by one rank (to a maximum of 10th rank when you reach 20th level).
name: Order of the Gate
